channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Score a premium Intel Core i9 Laptop for only $510 ahead of Memorial Day — and the CPU inside the Hasee X5 almost rivals the Apple M5 in the MacBook Pro
item.description: One of the best value-for-money laptops of 2025 makes a comeback in 2026

item.title: What to Do in LA if You’re Here for Business (2025)
item.description: A tech industry guide to where to stay, eat, work, and play while visiting LA.

item.title: Who will benefit most from SpaceX IPO? Mostly Elon — and a few from his inner circle
item.description: Elon Musk has the largest stake in SpaceX by billions of shares. The other biggest shareholders also have longstanding and deep ties to Musk.

item.title: ‘Creepy’ Listening Tool for Targeted Ads Didn’t Actually Work, FTC Says
item.description: Three firms will pay nearly $1 million for selling “Active Listening” technology that they claimed tapped people’s phones for advertising. The FTC alleges the “tech” was just pricey email lists.

item.title: Waymo halts freeway rides after robotaxis struggle in construction zones
item.description: The service suspension comes as Waymo has also paused operations in Atlanta and San Antonio after its robotaxis drove into flooded streets.

item.title: China unveils 'world's first' underwater data center — 2,000 server facility is powered by offshore wind, and cooled by the sea, making it one of the most efficient around
item.description: China launched an underwater AI data center using seawater cooling and offshore wind electricity to reduce rising infrastructure energy demands.

item.title: Starbucks abandons its AI inventory tool after only nine months
item.description: Looks like manual inventory management is back on the menu.

item.title: NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani takes to Twitch to chat with New Yorkers
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani is launching a Twitch series today at 4 p.m. ET.

item.title: High-end hi-fi company YBA has made what it claims is the world's first "transportable" CD player for SACDs — it's got the audiophiles in the office very excited, but it's not exactly a handheld model to slip in your jacket pocket
item.description: YBA has made what it claims is the world's first "transportable" CD player for Super Audio CDs, but it's not exactly a handheld model.

item.title: Nvidia really doesn't seem to care about gaming GPUs anymore — the company won't even bother to break down graphics sales in its big investor reports
item.description: There have been a few signs that Nvidia feels that gaming GPUs are less of a priority, and here's another.

item.title: Microsoft's Xbox controller refresh kills its underused accessory port
Meta Is in Crisis, Google Search’s Makeover, and AI Gets Booed by Graduates
item.description: In this episode of Uncanny Valley, we unpack the mass layoffs at Meta, big announcements at Google I/O, and the latest backlash against AI.

item.title: 'A true engineering feat': Silicon-based qubits have a clear advantage in race to million-qubit quantum computer thanks to tech that made Nvidia AI GPUs possible
item.description: Quantum computing using silicon-based qubits has a unique set of advantages versus other approaches
